commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `openclaw hooks relay` to wait for the direct native-hook bridge for the configured timeout before falling back to `nativeHook.invoke`, with CLI coverage for direct bridge and fallback paths.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +54. Total +55 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: with a missing direct bridge, the PR waits the full hook timeout before attempting `nativeHook.invoke`, while Codex enforces that same timeout around the hook subprocess. I did not run a live repro because this review is read-only and the contributor has not supplied real after-fix proof.

target.title: Prefer direct native hook relay before gateway fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- let `openclaw hooks relay` spend its configured hook timeout waiting for the direct localhost relay bridge before falling back to `nativeHook.invoke`
- add CLI coverage proving a live Codex hook relay uses the direct bridge and does not call the gateway RPC path
- keep gateway fallback coverage for unavailable direct bridges

## Why
In a self-hosted Codex harness setup, we saw hook delivery fall back to `nativeHook.invoke` and fail the gateway handshake with `token_missing`. The direct bridge is already the safer in-process path for current registrations; the previous 100ms registration grace could abandon it early under startup or event-loop delay and force an avoidable authenticated gateway call from the hook subprocess.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the change; it narrows the hook CLI toward the existing direct bridge path before using the legacy gateway fallback.
target.author: gmann14
